blocks.content: Siena steeled herself.
Shirone, having awakened the Immortal Function, might succumb to Overflow.
Etela believed he could overcome it, but if the worst happened, Shirone's life would be ruined.
Lost in thought, Siena barely noticed when Isthas stopped.
She entered without delay.
Walking down the hallway, she flung open the iron door labeled 'Supernatural Psychic Science Research Club.'
Through the haze of smoke, the kids sat unfazed, smiling leisurely.
The sight reignited her anger.
This was all too familiar.
The only differences were that the troublemakers she'd clashed with had all graduated—and Shirone had taken their place.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: 'Why do students change when they join this club? Even Shirone.'
Normally, Shirone would greet her with a bright smile, but now he maintained a perfect poker face.
It was expected, but still hurt.
Neid sat with his legs crossed arrogantly, while Iruki lounged in his chair, head tilted back.
Their dismissive aura was palpable.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: No greetings for your teacher? You little….

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: We were deep in discussion. Welcome.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: 'Hmph. School is school, and the club is the club, huh?'
But their arrogance ended today.
The faculty had approved the club's dissolution, and Etela, the advisor, had agreed.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: So, what brings you here, Professor? We've been keeping quiet lately.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Official notice about the club. I came to deliver it personally.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Then come in. It's humble, but…
Neid brushed off a spot on the sofa.
His demeanor showed no guilt.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Thanks. How about some tea?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Would you trust tea from me? You know no one can find this place once Isthas moves.
Siena raised an eyebrow, amused.
She knew this shabby warehouse didn't even have cold water, let alone tea.
Though their antics were irritating, their wit made it hard to dislike them.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Fair point. I suppose even I'd be wary. Let's get to it, then. I'd like you to vacate the lab within a month.
Neid's eyes bulged.
The bolt-from-the-blue demand left him speechless.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What? Vacate? Why?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Paperwork review showed you failed to meet the minimum membership this semester. The school already cut funding. So, clean up within a month.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Wait! Shirone joined! Three members qualify as an official club!

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: But you've done zero activities. If you want to save the club, produce research results within the month. Though I doubt you can in 'supernatural psychic science.'

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: That's unfair! Clubs don't have to hold presentations!

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: This is special. The lab's location in Isthas, your notorious reputation—few clubs are as inactive as yours. The one-month grace period is only because Professor Etela pleaded. Be grateful.
Shirone was stunned.
He'd just started growing fond of the club, and now it was being disbanded.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: But there's one way to save the club.
Neid leaned forward eagerly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: How? What do we need to do?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Withdraw Shirone. Then I'll turn a blind eye until next semester.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Whaaat?
As expected, Neid shot to his feet.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: No way! We didn't force Shirone to join—we're friends who stick together everywhere. Why tear us apart?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: If it's about my skipped classes, don't worry. We each have our own study methods.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I agree. I know the club's reputation and that Iruki and Neid aren't diligent in class. But they're doing their best in their fields. They're not wasting time. Please reconsider.
Neid hoped Siena's fondness for Shirone might sway her.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: It's no use. Shirone, this decision is also for your sake.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: For me? I'm doing my best.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Shirone, what's happening to you lately?
Shirone's heart sank.
Neid and Iruki averted their eyes.
Siena's intuition was right.
They knew everything.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You're terrible. Especially you, Iruki. You knew Shirone could fall into Overflow. Why didn't you tell me?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Iruki. What's Overflow?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's… nothing much. Just a phenomenon when you awaken beyond your current level. I had it at eight.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Overflow can shatter your mind. Etela told me about the Immortal Function. If you miss the timing, recovery might be impossible.
Shirone bit his lip.
He'd been having nightmares and hallucinations nightly.
Sometimes, he worried he'd lose his mind.
If this continued, there were no guarantees.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How was it for you?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm. Mine was nothing. A bit scary, though. When I told my dad, he said, 'You've always been like this. Just live with it.'
Siena was appalled.
That was all the comfort he'd offered his crying son?
But given who Iruki's father was, it made sense.
His neglect had undoubtedly shaped Iruki's eccentricity.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Desperate, Neid. As a friend, what do you think?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hahaha. Dunno? I've never had Overflow.
Hoping their experiences would change Shirone's mind, Siena sighed.
At least she'd impressed the danger upon him.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Anyway, Shirone will withdraw. If you care about him, accept it. His mental state is the priority.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Isn't that your judgment? I disagree.
Frustrated, Siena exhaled.
Insisting despite knowing their friend's condition wasn't friendship.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You don't know Overflow's terror. Iruki would understand.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Nope. I'm with Neid. It's Shirone's choice. Why are you so invested?
The cold reply seemed unfriendly, but it was precisely why they were friends.
Shirone wasn't upset—their rationality aligned.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: So you're fine if Shirone goes mad?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No reason not to be. But I believe Shirone can overcome it. If you aim for the top in this field, it's a risk you take.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Same here. Honestly, aren't you favoring Shirone too much? We don't mind, but he might find it burdensome.
After a pause, Neid's eyes gleamed.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Rumor has it you see Shirone as a man.
Of course, no such rumor existed.
But this was the most effective way to rattle a teacher.
Siena wasn't easily shaken—years of clashes with underground clubs had hardened her.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Suppose it's true. Then I can take Shirone now, right?
As a teacher, she couldn't forcibly withdraw a student unless they violated rules.
The club president's consent was crucial.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Stop forcing it. So you admit the rumor?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Think what you want. I won't back down.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Really? Then can we date too?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: No. Never.
Siena recoiled.
Neid's jab hit home.
Siena swallowed hard.
Perhaps Iruki was right.
The darkness that had once consumed her left deeper scars than Overflow.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: This seems to be my issue.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm fine. It's scary, but I'm more curious. What will become of me? Can I overcome it? Even if I fall into Overflow, I'd never blame you.
Siena was reminded of bittersweet memories.
The person who'd saved her from hell had said the same.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Siena. Face what's coming head-on. No matter how scary, don't look away. Closing your eyes won't make it disappear.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Go away! You don't understand!

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: 'If only I'd had that courage…'
